title: cassine eucleiformis
intro: Cassine eucleiformis, also known as Cassine, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It is a shrub or small tree known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is valued for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Cassine include its ability to reduce fever, treat respiratory infections, and alleviate digestive issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Cassine is often prepared for herbal use through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be consumed as teas, capsules, or applied topically as creams or ointments.
